effort_complexity.rationale: Replace driftGuard's broken comparison with a real re-render-and-diff; surface as a sworn doctor check.

design_decisions.choice: Byte-for-byte comparison of rendered index.md against the committed file, no whitespace/line-ending normalisation.
design_decisions.rationale: index.md is a build artifact per ADR-0009; any hand-edit at all, even whitespace-only, is exactly the drift class this guard exists to catch. Narrow, reversible (comparison strategy is local to checkRenderDrift, not a schema or contract surface).

design_decisions.choice: Remove driftGuard (internal/board/board.go) and its WriteBoard call site entirely, with no transition period, rather than leaving it running alongside the new doctor check.
design_decisions.rationale: driftGuard is advisory-only, already broken (re-parses raw index.md via the pattern this release removes), and would give false confidence if left in place as a second, weaker mechanism. Narrow, reversible (a pure deletion of dead code + its single call site).

violations: Gate 3b (AC-05 not satisfied): a fresh `sworn doctor` run at this worktree's HEAD (3679145) reports [ERROR] render drift (2026-07-01-render-drift-reconciliation) — this release's own committed index.md does not match board.Render's output. Root cause: commit b96f27a set S01's status.json state to 'implemented' without re-rendering index.md (last rendered in f0792d2 while S01 was still in_progress). AC-05 requires zero drift errors for T1-T5-scope releases beyond tracked exceptions #44/#45; this release is in scope and is not a tracked exception.

Gate 4/Gate 7 (stale reachability artefact / delivered claim): reachability-doctor-output.txt and proof.json's AC-05 delivered entry claim zero render-drift errors beyond the two tracked exceptions, captured in the same commit (b96f27a) that introduced the drift above. A fresh run at HEAD reports 3 drifted releases, not 2.

Gate 2 (undocumented touchpoint mismatch): spec.json lists internal/board/board_test.go as a planned touchpoint; it was never modified in this slice's diff, and neither proof.json's not_delivered (empty) nor its divergence section explains the omission.
